sources.label: Investing.com - Japanese stock surging on positive humanoid robot order outlook (pre-Jun 6 context)
Kabutan 6324 news feed (no new disclosures Jun 6-9; AGM Jun 19)
Simply Wall St - Harmonic Drive margin compression vs bullish growth narratives

verdicts.rankRationale: Yesterday's verdict already demoted the name for crowding and post-rerating chase; no new disclosure since June 6 and today's -1.8% drift is too small to restore asymmetry after +83% YTD, so hold at the freshly lowered rank with mild negative drift.
verdicts.bull: Near-monopoly in high-end strain-wave gears with humanoid-related orders guided to ~JPY2.5B in FY3/26 and management flagging a potential double-to-triple in FY3/27 as US prototype programs scale; March-quarter orders hit a four-year high.
verdicts.neutral: The May results, 14% sales-growth/9% OPM guidance, and the 2026-2030 AI-robot growth plan are all known and priced; at $4.2B cap and +83% YTD this is the consensus way to play humanoid actuation, leaving little hidden alpha despite a sound thesis.
verdicts.bear: Operating margin is still single-digit and humanoid orders are ~1% of the market cap narrative; any prototype-schedule slip, cheaper Chinese strain-wave competition, or broader AI/robotics derating compresses the multiple far faster than fundamentals can fill it.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Concrete FY3/27 humanoid order numbers or a named US mass-production design win (next checkpoint: Q1 FY3/27 results and the June 19 AGM commentary).
verdicts.primaryRisk: Crowded post-rerating positioning unwinding on a humanoid sentiment wobble, plus Chinese harmonic-reducer price competition eroding the pricing-power premium.
